response.justification: The candidate is an experienced generalist with a strong web and full-stack background, but he misses nearly every core requirement of this role: he lists no Japanese proficiency (native-level is required), no embedded LAN-switch or networking-protocol work, no security technologies such as MACsec or 802.1X, and no Linux kernel source modification experience. His C/C++ exposure is a brief stint at National Instruments a decade ago, far from the strong C-for-UNIX proficiency the role demands.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career spanning web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded/hardware-adjacent work
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang microservices handling millions of requests per second)
Strong software craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools, and active knowledge sharing
concerns: No Japanese language ability listed, failing a hard requirement (native-level Japanese)
No experience with L2/L3 switching, MACsec, 802.1X, SNMP/NETCONF, or other required security and networking technologies
C for UNIX and Linux kernel experience is minimal and dated; recent work is TypeScript/Node.js product development

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security-protocol experience
Insufficient C/UNIX and Linux kernel depth
